title: Keep the type system sound
description: Ensure type rules reject every ill-typed program (no false accepts) so well-typed programs cannot go wrong at runtime in the ways the type system promises.
body: Use when implementing or changing type-checking rules. Do not relax a rule to make a specific program compile if it lets a class of unsafe programs through, and do not conflate convenience coercions with soundness. Actions: derive each rule from the type system's specification, prove progress/preservation hold for new constructs, and test with both well-typed programs (must accept) and ill-typed ones (must reject). Validation signals include a test suite where every ill-typed sample is rejected with a clear error and no well-typed sample is wrongly rejected. Risk boundary: an unsound rule that accepts an ill-typed program breaks the language's safety guarantee for everyone; treat soundness holes as blocking and escalate before weakening a rule.
